title_en: LEGO Just Released a Stacked Lineup of New Sets From Every Major Franchise for June 2026
summary_en: It's the start of summer and LEGO is going all out with new sets from all of its major franchises.
body_en:
LEGO has consistently been releasing new sets throughout 2026 , but the June lineup is built different. It's the start of summer, and LEGO is going all out with new sets from all of its major franchises and a few new ones as well. This month we're getting the first-ever Shrek Sets, the first Peanuts set, and a massive new Lord of the Rings build. If you're a fan of LEGO sets for adults, you'll definitely want to check out all of the new sets that were just made available.
If you want a curated selection of what's available this month, you can scroll sideways through the catalog of top picks above. For details about each of the categories and standout sets for June, read on.
The LEGO SEGA Genesis Console is this year's retro gaming recreation, and it looks awesome. At only $39.99, it's even cheaper than the Game Boy set from last year and will absolutely fill you with nostalgia for a lost generation of gaming.
LEGO is offering up only one new Architecture set this month, but it's a big one. We've previously gotten a more basic New York Skyline set, but this version is much larger and much grander. It's a bigger version of the New York skyline amid a starry backdrop in the shape of a big apple.
There's only one new Jurassic World set coming out this month, but it's pretty awesome. LEGO released the massive T-Rex fossil set last year and the triceratops is the obvious follow-up to that.
There has never been a LEGO Peanuts set, but that doesn't mean that there isn't room for one in the June lineup. The LEGO Snoopy set went up for preorder all the way back in February 2026 and is finally releasing this month. There are multiple ways to display this set and absolutely no Charlie Brown included.
The first-ever LEGO Shrek sets were announced back in April and they look great. There's already a ton of Shrek merch out there, but now you can build the Ogre and his trusty steed ahead of Shrek 5 if you really want to.
June is always a big month for Harry Potter sets , but perhaps the most intriguing set is the Dumbledore's office book nook. LEGO released the bulk of its book nook sets last June, including a Hogwarts Express version, and has been pretty quiet about more of this type of set since then.
If you love the Fast and Furious movies , the good news is there are now more LEGO sets that can help you show that! There are three new builds that accurately represent some of the famous cars from those films that are now available.
There are a ton of new LEGO Disney sets for kids available this month as well as an incredible new offering for adults. The Disney Main Street set is a massive $399.99 build you'll be able to buy starting on June 4.
June is a pretty slow month for Star Wars sets coming off some pretty exciting new releases in May . There are only two new sets, including a giant Darth Vader minifigure that is already on backorder at the LEGO Store .
June has a nice mix of new sets for fans of Marvel, both as adults and kids. The most prominent new release is the massive S.H.I.E.L.D. Helicarrier you'll likely recognize from the first Avengers movie .
There are quite a few new LEGO Bluey sets available for both very little kids and slightly older kids. As someone with a niece who absolutely loves this show, I'll definitely be looking at the new releases for future Christmas gift ideas.
Minecraft continues to be one of the most popular LEGO themese available for kids, but there's a couple of display sets out this month that look great for adults. Both The Skeleton and Endor Dragon are classic mobs from the game and would look excellent on any desk or shelf.
Although Fortnite isn't nearly as popular as it used to be, LEGO is still producing more new sets in collaboration with the game. Each of these sets comes with some in-game item or outfit you can only get when you buy the set.
Jacob Kienlen is a Senior Audience Development Strategist and Writer for IGN. Born and raised in Portland, Oregon, he has considered the Northwest his home for his entire life. With a bachelor's degree in communication and 10 years of professional writing experience, his expertise is spread across a variety of different pop culture topics -- from TV series to books and the latest Pokémon games .
